_comment_location_aliases: Maps freeform campaign-export location labels to the canonical store names in the sales sheet's By Location 2.0 tab, so per-location marketing-spend-% joins.

Pico-Robertson.overrides: moved Top→Mid 2026-06-12 (Maxx): Bev Hills reopen will steal volume — fund to defend/backfill during redistribution

Midtown West.overrides: SUMMER-ONLY window 2026-07-14 to 2026-09-07 (Maxx, Labor Day per roadmap.window_end) — raise to 3-4% spend, then REVERT to 'keep spend ~0, protect'. Biggest single location by total sales (~$164K/wk) with effectively $0 UE Ads presence (W28: only 2 DD offer campaigns, $940 spend, ~2% spend%) — needs a new UE Ads campaign stood up to reach 3-4%, doesn't exist yet. Do not treat as a permanent tier change.
Midtown West.reorder_note: 31.9% as 'NYC' — live Apr 18, not comparable; revisit 60-90d

Larchmont.overrides: moved Top→Mid 2026-06-12 (Maxx): Bev Hills reopen will steal volume — fund to defend/backfill during redistribution
Larchmont.reorder_note: 45.2% — ONLY retention outlier (floor of industry healthy); retention-first, investigate before major acquisition

South Bay.overrides: promoted Mid→Top 2026-06-12: 8.8x at 6% — trim into 0-3% band

San Jose.overrides: GM override of diagnostic Red — deliberate growth program (May 16 tweak); no hard tier ceiling as of 2026-07-14 (Maxx), gated by 10% portfolio guardrail instead — see tiers.Low.notes, fund acquisition here first (organic headroom)

Pasadena.overrides: GM override of diagnostic Red — paired with San Jose program; no hard tier ceiling as of 2026-07-14 (Maxx), gated by 10% portfolio guardrail instead — see tiers.Low.notes, fix efficiency (retire legacy campaigns) before adding spend, don't just trim

Beverly Hills.overrides: REOPENED — geo-capped 10-12%, Lapsed win-back; overlaps Pico/Larchmont
Beverly Hills.reorder_note: 82.7% pre-closure (UE 82.8/GH 81.7) — understates loyalty; expect pent-up return surge; clean read 30d post-reopen

Top.notes: loyalty/retention lean; SM stepped-pullback test P2; Midtown West holds ~$0

Mid.notes: location SLs + tiered SxSy New offers; re-order tilt deferred (no data captured)

Low.notes: SJ/Pas: Maxx 2026-07-14 (re: Kelly Burke top-line-growth ask) — NO hard ceiling on Low tier itself; band can flex above 15% if needed and efficient (ROAS holding), gated by the PORTFOLIO blended guardrail (now 10%, see portfolio_guardrail below), not a tier-level cap or a date-based taper. Still an active acquisition + repeat/retention program, NOT hold. W28 actual: 10.6% blended for the group. San Jose (8% spend, 42% mkt-driven-sales vs 54.6% tier avg) has real organic headroom — fund acquisition there first. Pasadena (12% spend, ROAS -17.6% L4W, W28 dashboard flags ⚠ Pull back) — retire/rebuild inefficient legacy campaigns (e.g. Pasadena SL 2.5x) before adding spend; efficiency gate applies per-store even though the tier band itself isn't capped. GAP: neither store has a dedicated repeat/retention (Lapsed win-back or loyalty) campaign — both entries are acquisition-only; needed to actually 'drive repeat behavior' per Maxx's ask. New spend must be New/Lapsed-segmented, not All-customer (see cannibalization note below).

New.notes: 4-8wk ramp then taper; Beverly Hills exception: capped 10-12%, geo-tightened (Pico/Larchmont overlap), Lapsed win-back

Red.notes: none currently — SJ/Pas overridden to Low by GM 2026-06-11

portfolio_guardrail.note: raised 6%→10% 2026-07-14 (Maxx). Portfolio-wide ceiling, not per-tier: individual tiers (incl. Low/SJ+Pas) can run above their own band if needed and efficient, as long as the whole-portfolio blend stays <=10%.

growth_targets.note: W28 actual $1.41M. Ramp to $1.5M/wk, spend held to <=10% blended and efficient (see portfolio_guardrail) — growth from efficiency gains + reallocation, not just more budget.

location_priority.target: $100K/wk each, comp-based (Pico-Robertson $81K, Silver Lake $80K, South Bay $79K already near/above that on Mid-tier budgets)
see tiers.Low — efficiency-gated, San Jose funded first (organic headroom), Pasadena fixed first (retire legacy campaigns)

tier_strategy.reorder_rates: CANONICAL = May 8 2026 analysis (Notion 35ad3ff0…): portfolio 74.5% blended, retention-rich. Jun 12 capture INVALIDATED (truncated exports, n=1-63, contradicted by May 8 full pulls). Quarterly re-confirm; next at Aug re-tier incl. BH 30d-post-reopen + NYC 60-90d reads
tier_strategy.holiday_scheme: MDW precedent (May 22-25): Spend $20 Save $5 portfolio-wide CA, all customers, ~11x blended. Repeat July 4 wknd + Labor Day wknd.

client_forecast.caveat: WHOLE-RESTAURANT net sales (lunch+dinner, all channels incl 1P) — NOT directly comparable to our 3P Total Sales levels; use trajectory/YoY direction + demand notes (school calendars), not levels

p6_reads.shape: every store declines W1→W5 (~-15-25%); trough = July 4 week — forecasted demand dip, aligns with clean-baseline exclusion
p6_reads.note: client PRICES IN Robertson/Larchmont declines — beating her forecast = defense success bar

threeP_share.validated: portfolio reconciliation W23(=P6W1): our 3P $1,455K / Kelly whole-restaurant $1,751K = 83% — confirms our 'Total Sales' IS 3P-only and the 75-80% holds (we run slightly hot of it)
threeP_share.caveat: single-week (W23) per-store ratios are noisy; portfolio 83% is the solid number. Refine to L4W. >100% stores = beating her total forecast OR she's conservative; confirm over P7.
